rulings.Case Title: EMILY DAVIDSON VS. LINDA SUSAN GORDAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Toc Ompel Discovery Related To The Sudden Medical Emergency Defense
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 26, 2011, line 2. PLAINTIFF EMILY DAVIDSON'S Motion To Compel Discovery Related To The Sudden Medical Emergency Defense. Plaintiff's motion to compel is granted in part. Defendant is required to respond to request for production of documents 5, 6, 7, and 8 for the past 5 years of medical records and all medical records evidencing any neurological symptoms or disorders. Defendant must also provide psychological or psychiatric records for 5 years prior to the accident. All will be subject to a protective order. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: YASMEEN HAMID VS. BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 26, 2011, line 4. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Motion to Strike the prayer for punitive damages denied. Plaintiff has sufficient pled facts to support this prayer. Defendant¿s Motion to Strike the prayer for injunctive relief is denied based on the reasons articulated in the ruling on the Demurrer. Defendant¿s Motion to Strike the prayer for Attorney Fees is denied. Paragraph 28 of the FAC is sufficient to support this prayer for relief. Defendant¿s Motion to Strike the prayer for non-economic damages is denied. Because the cause of action for conversion stands, so does the prayer for non-economic damages. Erlich v. Menzies (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 553-54. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: YASMEEN HAMID VS. BANK OF AMERICA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 26, 2011, line 4. DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 1st cause of action (Conversion) is overruled. ¿Where the relationship of debtor and creditor only exists, conversion of the funds representing the indebtedness will not lie against the debtor, unless he holds the deposit in a fiduciary capacity, and is bound to return to the owner the identical money.¿ Watson v. Stockton Morris Plan Co. (1939) 34 Cal.App.2d 393, 403. The relationship between Plaintiff and Defendant is more that that of a mere debtor and creditor and a claim for conversion has been adequately pled. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action (Violation of UCL) is overruled. Plaintiff has stated a cause of action for violation of the UCL. If Plaintiff can show that a proper demand was made and Defendant did not release the funds, it was violating a court order and thereby acting unlawfully. Further, while Defendant correctly argues that the FAC contains no allegations that the alleged past wrongs will continue or be repeated as to Plaintiff, plaintiff has pled facts that end to show that the alleged past wrongs may continue or be repeated. Finally, as to the preemption argument, although the Dodd-Frank was enacted in 2011, after the event which gave rise to the FAC, since an injunction can only alter future actions, Dodd-Frank applies and the injunctive relief is not preempted. Defendant¿s Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action (negligence) is sustained without leave to amend. Although the existence of the court order was sufficient to make a relationship between Plaintiff and Defendant more than a mere debtor and creditor, it is not sufficient to impose a fiduciary relationship. Defendant was obligated to abide by the court order, but was under no legal obligation to act on behalf and for the benefit of Plaintiff. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN W. VICAN, INDIVIDUALLY ON BEHALF OF VS. CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 26, 2011, line 11. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIANS' SERVICE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. While plaintiff argues there is only one breach of contract, the complaint involves multiple causes of action because multiple contracts are at issue. The plain reading of the '07 contract shows that there is a location requirement. Accordingly, plaintiff must amend to remove reference to the '07 contract. =(302/LMG)
